name: TiKV
description: Open-source, distributed, transactional key-value database built in Rust, originally created to complement TiDB. CNCF graduated project.

hosting-options.comment: Self-hosted is primary deployment model. No dedicated TiKV managed service exists, but TiDB Cloud (which uses TiKV) provides managed hosting. Third-party managed via TiDB Cloud.

initial-release.comment: First version released alongside TiDB in 2016, development started in 2015

topology.comment: Uses Raft consensus with leader election per Region (shard). Each Region has a leader that handles writes.

sharding.comment: Data is partitioned into Regions by key range. Regions automatically split and merge based on size thresholds. Placement Driver (PD) handles automatic data migration.

replication-factor.comment: Default is 3 replicas per Region, configurable

multi-region.comment: Supports geo-replication with configurable placement rules

cap-position.comment: Prioritizes consistency using Raft consensus. Writes require majority acknowledgment.

consistency-model.comment: Strong consistency via Raft. Linearizable reads from leader. Serializable isolation through Percolator-based transactions.

acid-transactions.comment: Full ACID compliance with distributed transactions using Percolator-inspired two-phase commit

transaction-scope.comment: Supports distributed transactions across multiple Regions (partitions) using two-phase commit

isolation-levels.comment: Provides snapshot isolation (SI) by default. Also supports snapshot isolation with lock (SELECT ... FOR UPDATE) and externally consistent transactions.

distributed-transactions.comment: Cross-node transaction coordination via Percolator-style two-phase commit protocol

query-language.comment: Pure key-value API. No SQL interface - TiKV provides raw KV and transactional KV APIs. For SQL, use TiDB which sits on top of TiKV.

sql-compatibility.comment: TiKV itself has no SQL interface. SQL is provided by TiDB layer.

secondary-indexes.comment: As a key-value store, TiKV doesn't have built-in secondary index support. Indexes are managed at the TiDB layer.

joins.comment: Key-value store with no join support at the TiKV layer

aggregations.comment: Has coprocessor framework for pushing down computations, but no native aggregation API. Aggregations handled by TiDB layer.

max-cluster-size.comment: Designed to scale to thousands of nodes. Official benchmarks use 3-12 nodes. No specific maximum tested cluster size documented.

write-throughput.comment: Varies significantly based on hardware and workload. YCSB benchmarks show ~15K insert ops/sec, ~7K update ops/sec per node.

read-latency-p99.comment: Suitable for latency-sensitive services. Average latency <10ms even at high pressure. P99 varies with workload and cluster size.

auto-scaling.comment: Automatic data rebalancing via Placement Driver (PD). Can scale out by adding nodes; PD automatically redistributes Regions.

hot-spot-handling.comment: Automatic Region splitting when size exceeds threshold. PD handles load balancing and automatic rebalancing.

zero-downtime-upgrades.comment: Rolling upgrade strategy. Components upgraded one at a time with leader eviction before stopping each instance.

online-schema-changes.comment: Not applicable - TiKV is schema-less as a key-value store. Schema changes are handled at the TiDB layer.

point-in-time-recovery.comment: Supported via BR (Backup & Restore) tool with snapshot + log backup. PITR available since v6.2.0 for RawKV.

backup-strategy.comment: Full snapshot backup, incremental log backup, and continuous backup for PITR. Supports S3, GCS, Azure Blob, NFS.

kubernetes-operator.comment: TiKV Operator exists but is deprecated. Recommended to use TiDB Operator which manages TiKV as a component.

rust-driver.comment: Official tikv/client-rust crate. Noted as not yet production-ready with unstable APIs.

go-driver.comment: Official client-go, most stable and feature-complete driver

dotnet-driver.comment: No official or known community .NET driver

python-driver.comment: Official client-py built on Rust client via PyO3. Proof-of-concept stage, requires Python 3.6+.

ruby-driver.comment: No official or known community Ruby driver

c-cpp-driver.comment: C client exists but noted as early development stage

orm-support.comment: Not applicable - TiKV is a key-value store without SQL interface. ORMs work with TiDB layer.

free-tier.comment: No dedicated TiKV managed service. TiDB Cloud (which uses TiKV) has a free tier but is primarily for TiDB.

pricing-model.comment: Via TiDB Cloud: pricing based on vCPUs, storage, and hourly compute. No standalone TiKV managed service.

cost-estimate-small.comment: Estimated from TiDB Cloud pricing: 3x 8vCPU TiKV nodes at ~$1.37/hr each ≈ $140/month compute only, plus storage.

change-data-capture.comment: TiKV-CDC available for RawKV since v6.2.0. TiCDC available for transactional API via TiDB stack.

watch-subscribe.comment: TiKV-CDC supports subscribing to data changes on RawKV for real-time replication

triggers.comment: No database-side triggers - TiKV is a key-value store without stored procedure support

event-streaming.comment: TiCDC supports sinking to Kafka. TiKV-CDC provides custom streaming protocol for cross-cluster replication.

stackoverflow-tags.comment: Stack Overflow tag exists but question count is very low. Community primarily uses ask.pingcap.com forum.
